qt: R & D department of a toy manufacturing company has developed a report suggesting the company to diversify its production line into stuff toys. The cost incurred on this report is known as:
Which of the following is a method of “capital budgeting”?

Contribution margin per unit is Rs. 60; sold units are 1,000 and fixed cost is Rs. 50,000. 
Required: Identify the profit/loss with the help of provided data.
You have decided to use present value method to a proposed project. What is the condition for acceptance of the project?

ABC Company has currently option to work on seven projects but only four projects are with positive Net Present Value (NPV). Total cost to opt these four projects is Rs. six million but the management of the company has imposed limit of Rs. four million for these projects.  
Keep into consideration the above mentioned restriction, this situation is known as:
In case of IRR the NPV……………. is equal to ZERO, while in case of MIRR the NPV of terminal cash flows………….. is equal to investment.

It is assumed that the initial investment for “Project B” is Rs. 170,000. Cash flows are Rs. 40,000, Rs. 90,000, and Rs. 40,000 in the first, second third year respectively. 
Required: Identify the payback period of Project B.

Following information has been extracted from ABC Company to find out the manufacturing cost per unit. 
Direct Material: Rs. 6,520 per unit; Direct Labor: Rs. 3,000 per unit; over head cost: Rs. 1,500 per unit.

Following information has been extracted for the year to calculate the capital turnover of RC Company.
Average invested capital:  Rs. 340,000 and Net income: Rs. 250,000 and Sales revenue: Rs.700,000
Find the capital turnover with the help of provided information.
op: Required more data to calculate the capital turnover

qt: Which of the following is a basic characteristic of capital budgeting?
op: Determine worth of long term investment

qt: Which of the following is not a weakness of payback period method?
op: Ignores the Time Value of Money
Ignores CFs occurring after payback period

qt: Which of the following is (are) objective(s) of pricing?
op: To know the theoretical economic background to pricing.
To be able to use cost-plus pricing.
To know the differences between full cost pricing, rate of marginal pricing.

qt:
Following information has been extracted for the year to calculate the Residual Income of XYZ Company.
Cost of Capital:  Rs. 340,000 and Net income: Rs. 500,000 and Sles: Rs.750, 000
Find the Residual Income with the help of provided information.

Present value of outflows= Rs. 365,500
Present value of inflows= Rs. 269,300
Profitability index= ?
The probability for economic recession in a given country is 0.2 and the NPV of a project under consideration by an organization there is -500,000 Rs. What is Expected Net Present value for it?
Which of the following is correct for Economic Value Added (EVA)?
op: EVA = Net operating profit after tax – capital charge
EVA = Net operating profit after tax + Total investment
EVA = Net operating profit after tax – Total investment
EVA = Net operating profit after tax + capital charge

qt:
Division Y of automobile spare parts; manufactures motors and budgets to transfer 72 motors to Division Z and to sell 48 motors to external customers. The standard cost information per motor for Division Y is as follows:
Variable cost per motor: Rs. 100 per motor; fixed production overhead and fixed selling & administrative overhead per motor: Rs. 150.  In order to set the external selling price the company uses a 44 % mark up on total standard cost.
Required: Identify the sales if the transfer price is set at marginal cost.
Which of the following statement is correct for “Residual Income”?
op: Earned income – expenses for the period

qt: Which of the following is an advantage of IRR?
op: Results are expressed as a simple percentage
It indicates how sensitive decisions are to a change in interest rates
It takes into account the time value of money

qt: In case of mutually exclusive; when “Project M” should be accepted?

Investment required for the project= Rs.300,000
Cash flows= Rs.80,000, Rs. 160,000 and Rs.180,000
Payback period= ?

For stock of ABC company:
Standard deviation=8%
Expected return=16%
Cost of capital=10%
Coefficient of variation= ?
Which of the following is (are) advantage(s) of Gantt chart?
op: It does not give a clear indication of interrelationship between the separate activities.
It does not clearly indicate details regarding the progress of activities.
It provides an easy graphical presentation of when activities might take place.

qt: An investment opportunity is offering you a rate of 15.5%. There is an inflation of 5.5%. What is the real rate which you can earn?
Which of the following is correct for Cost plus pricing?
op: It is short run pricing concept.
It is useful for any time duration.
It is long run pricing concept.

qt:
Contribution margin per unit is Rs. 60; sold units are 1,000 and fixed cost is Rs. 50,000. 
Required: Identify the total contribution margin with the help of provided data.

op: Cash flows of the project are conventional.
Cash flows of the project are unconventional.
Initial cash outflow is very high.

qt:

op: Required more data to find value

qt: IRR takes into account………………………….., which is good basis for decision making.
You have decided to use present value method to a proposed project. What is the condition for rejection of the project?

Identify the discounted payback period of “Project A” of a Company with the help of following data: 
Cash outflow for the project: Rs. 2,800,000; discounted cash flows for year 1, year 2, year 3, year 4 are  Rs. 1,835,600 ; Rs. 1,106,500; Rs. 473,200 and Rs. 323,900 respectively.

Division Y of automobile spare parts; manufactures motors and budgets to transfer 72 motors to Division Z and to sell 48 motors to external customers. The standard cost information per motor for Division Y is as follows:
Variable cost per motor: Rs. 100 per motor; fixed production overhead and fixed selling & administrative overhead per motor: Rs. 150. In order to set the external selling price the company uses a 44 % mark up on total standard cost.
Required: Identify the sales if the transfer price is set at the total production cost.

Variable cost= Rs.1300
Fixed cost= Rs.310,000
Break-even point (units)= ?
Project should be accepted for investment if:
op: weighted average cost of capital >cost of debt
weighted average cost of capital < internal rate of return
weighted average cost of capital = internal rate of return
weighted average cost of capital > internal rate of return

qt: Which of the following is not a method of “capital budgeting”?

Present value of outflows= Rs. 165,500
Present value of inflows= Rs. 269,300
Profitability index= ?
Residual Income is evaluation measure of which of the following?
Which of the following is most appropriate for Economic Value Added (EVA)?
op: It measures the Economic performance of division.
It measures the operating expenses of division.
It measures the Accounting profit of division.
It measures the net income of division.

qt:

Cost of Capital:  Rs. 340,000 and Net income: Rs. 250,000 and Sales revenue: Rs.700,000

Required: Identify the payback period of Project A.
Project with unconventional cash flows can have either ………………or ………………… IRR.
Which of the following is not an advantage of IRR?
op: Results are expressed as a simple percentage
It takes into account the time value of money
It is easily understood as compare to other methods
Unconventional cash flows can have negative IRR

qt:
Following information has been extracted for the year to find the Return on Investment of ABC Company.
Return: Rs. 590,000; investment: Rs. 4,690,000 and Sales: Rs. 4,900,000
Find the Return on Investment with the help of provided information.

op: Give critical path and slack time

qt: Which of the following statement is correct for “Project Evaluation & Review Technique”?
op: It shows the inter relationship of various jobs which makeup the overall project and clearly identify the critical path of the project.
It focuses on successful completion of program in the shortest possible time.
It was developed by US navy for planning and controlling of Polaris missile program.

qt: Cost based transfer pricing is appropriate:
op: Whenever profit responsibility is centralized and each division is operated on a cost center basis.
Whenever profit responsibility is decentralized and each division is operated on a cost center basis.
Whenever profit responsibility is centralized and each division is operated on a profit center basis.
Whenever profit responsibility is decentralized and each division is operated on a standard cost basis.

qt: A project is considered as……………. if the cash flows of one are unaffected by the acceptance of the other.

Required: At what point Product D should sale and why?
op: Further processing on product D causes higher incremental sales revenue in this case
It should be sold at split off point because additional processing cost is higher than incremental sales revenue.
More information is required to identify the reason
It should be sold at split off point because additional processing cost is lesser than incremental costs.

Required: Identify Spending Variance with the help of provided information.
Which of the given formula is correct for “labor rate variance”?
op: (Actual hours worked) X (standard rate of per labor hour / actual rate of per labor hour)
(Actual hours worked) X (actual rate of per labor hour – standard rate of per labor hour)
(Actual hours worked) X (standard rate of per labor hour X actual rate of per labor hour)
(Actual hours worked) X (standard rate of per labor hour + actual rate of per labor hour)

qt: Fixed costs of XYZ company was of Rs. 2,000, and sold 30 units for Rs. 140 each. The total variable costs were Rs. 1,600. What is the net income or loss of the Company?

As per the information gathered by management of NS Mills, it was found that cost of manufacturing and cost of importing a certain component is exactly the same. Even then the management decided to manufacture the component.

op: Cost per unit increases as variable cost increase
Cost per unit decreases as fixed cost is same

qt:
An electric company produced and sold ceiling fans at Rs. 1,500 each by incurring the variable cost of Rs. 1,200 each and fixed cost of Rs. 300,000.

Which of the following is not a type of variance?

Which of the given is correct for “sales volume profit variance”?
op: (Actual unit sold +budgeted nits sold) X (Standard profit per unit)
(Actual unit sold +budgeted nits sold) / Standard profit per unit
(Actual unit sold –budgeted nits sold)  X (Standard profit per unit
(Actual unit sold –budgeted nits sold) / Standard profit per unit

qt: Which of the given formula is used to calculate activity ratio?
op: Standard hours produced ÷ Budgeted labor hours
Standard hours produced ÷ Actual labor hours worked
Actual labor hours worked ÷ Budgeted labor hours

qt: Which of the following would be considered as Relevant Cost?
op: The current disposal price of existing machinery

op: More information is required to find answer

qt: Which of the following is NOT feature of a relevant cost?

It causes an increment in cost

qt: Which of the given is correct for “variable overhead efficiency variance”?
op: (Absorbed variable overhead) – (Standard variable overhead at actual output)
(Absorbed variable overhead) + (Standard variable overhead at actual output)
(Absorbed variable overhead) – (Standard variable overhead at budgeted output)
(Absorbed variable overhead) + (Standard variable overhead at budgeted output)

qt: Which of the given formula is used to calculate efficiency ratio?
op: Standard hours produced ÷ Budgeted labor hours
Standard hours produced ÷ Actual labor hours worked
Actual labor hours worked ÷ Budgeted labor hours

op: There is no relation between liquidity and profitability.

op: It is treated as cash inflow in operating activity.

op: There is no relation between liquidity and profitability.

It is treated as cash outflow in financing activity.
